agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier context. A user (sender or potentially receiver) requests to cancel a previously placed delivery order. Cancellation has cascading effects on dispatch, driver assignment, package handling, billing, and refunds. The PM spec is extremely vague — it does not define WHO can cancel, WHEN cancellation is allowed, what constitutes an 'order,' or what happens to in-flight physical processes.","bounded_context":"Order Management — owns the order lifecycle from placement through completion or cancellation. Interfaces with Dispatch (driver assignment), Billing (charges/refunds), and Fulfillment (physical package handling).","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A request for courier pickup and delivery of a package from origin to destination, with a defined lifecycle (placed → confirmed → assigned → picked_up → in_transit → delivered | cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A user-initiated request to terminate an order before its terminal state (delivered). Not the same as a refund, which is a billing concern triggered by cancellation."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle phase of an order. Determines what operations are permitted, including whether cancellation is allowed and under what terms."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period during which cancellation is permitted without penalty. Typically before a driver has been dispatched or picked up the package."},{"term":"Cancellation Fee","definition":"A charge applied when cancellation occurs after work has already begun (e.g., driver dispatched, package picked up). Compensates for sunk operational cost."},{"term":"Requestor","definition":"The authenticated user initiating the cancellation. Must be the order owner (the user who placed the order)."},{"term":"Terminal State","definition":"A final, irreversible order status: 'delivered' or 'cancelled'. No further state transitions are permitted."},{"term":"In-Flight Order","definition":"An order where physical fulfillment has begun — a driver has been assigned, is en route to pickup, or has possession of the package."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Only the user who placed the order (order owner) may cancel it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary users to cancel others' orders is a data integrity and security violation. Ownership is a hard access-control constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given user A placed an order, when user B attempts to cancel it, then the system rejects the request with 'Forbidden'."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An order can only be cancelled if it has not yet reached a terminal state (delivered or already cancelled).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A delivered package has physically changed hands — cancellation of a completed real-world event is nonsensical. A cancelled order is already terminated. Violating this corrupts order state.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status 'delivered', when the owner requests cancellation, then the system rejects with 'Order cannot be cancelled in its current state'."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Cancellation of an order in status 'placed' or 'confirmed' (before driver assignment) incurs no cancellation fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Fee policy is a business decision that can change. A VP could decide to always charge or never charge. This is policy, not physics.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status 'confirmed', when the owner cancels, then no cancellation fee is applied and a full refund is issued."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Cancellation of an in-flight order (status 'assigned' or 'picked_up') incurs a cancellation fee proportional to work performed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Fee structure is a business policy. The specific fee amount or percentage is a configurable threshold.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status 'assigned', when the owner cancels, then a cancellation fee is deducted from the refund amount."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Cancellation of an order in status 'in_transit' is not permitted via self-service; it requires contacting support.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy to prevent logistical chaos with a package already moving. Policy could change if ops builds a real-time redirect capability.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status 'in_transit', when the owner attempts self-service cancellation, then the system rejects with a message directing the user to contact support."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A cancelled order must emit a domain event (OrderCancelled) so downstream systems (Dispatch, Billing, Notifications) can react.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without event emission, dispatch may send a driver to a cancelled pickup, billing may charge for a cancelled order, and the user gets no confirmation. System consistency depends on this.","testable_assertion":"Given a successful cancellation, then an OrderCancelled event is published containing order_id, cancelled_by, cancelled_at, cancellation_reason, and refund_amount."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The user must provide a cancellation reason from a predefined set (e.g., 'changed_mind', 'ordered_by_mistake', 'delivery_too_slow', 'other').","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Reason collection is a product/analytics requirement, not a system integrity concern. Could be made optional by product decision.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancellation request without a reason, then the system rejects with a validation error requiring a reason."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Cancellation is idempotent — cancelling an already-cancelled order returns success without side effects.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Non-idempotent cancellation could double-refund or emit duplicate events, corrupting financial records and downstream state.","testable_assertion":"Given an order already in status 'cancelled', when the owner requests cancellation again, then the system returns success with no new events emitted and no additional refund."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An authenticated user who owns an order in status 'placed'","when":"The user submits a cancellation request with a valid reason","then":"The order status transitions to 'cancelled', a full refund is initiated, an OrderCancelled event is emitted, and the user receives confirmation"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An authenticated user who owns an order in status 'confirmed'","when":"The user submits a cancellation request with a valid reason","then":"The order status transitions to 'cancelled', a full refund is initiated, an OrderCancelled event is emitted, and the user receives confirmation"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An authenticated user who owns an order in status 'assigned' (driver dispatched but package not yet picked up)","when":"The user submits a cancellation request with a valid reason","then":"The order status transitions to 'cancelled', the driver assignment is released via Dispatch, a refund minus the cancellation fee is initiated, and the user is shown the fee breakdown before confirming"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An authenticated user who owns an order in status 'picked_up'","when":"The user submits a cancellation request with a valid reason","then":"The order status transitions to 'cancelled', Dispatch is notified to instruct the driver to return the package, a refund minus a higher cancellation fee is initiated, and the user sees the fee breakdown before confirming"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An authenticated user who owns an order in status 'in_transit'","when":"The user attempts to cancel","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and displays a message: 'This order is in transit. Please contact support to request cancellation.' with a link/button to support"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An authenticated user who owns an order in status 'delivered'","when":"The user attempts to cancel","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and displays: 'This order has been delivered and cannot be cancelled.'"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An authenticated user who does NOT own the order","when":"The user attempts to cancel","then":"The system returns HTTP 403 Forbidden with no order details leaked"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An unauthenticated request","when":"A cancellation is attempted","then":"The system returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order in status 'assigned' or 'picked_up'","when":"The user initiates cancellation","then":"The system shows the cancellation fee amount and requires explicit confirmation before executing the cancellation (two-step process)"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order that has already been cancelled","when":"The owner submits another cancellation request","then":"The system returns success (200) with the existing cancellation details and does NOT emit a new event or initiate a duplicate refund"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A cancellation request missing the required reason field","when":"Submitted","then":"The system returns a 422 validation error specifying the reason field is required"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A successful cancellation of an order in status 'assigned'","when":"The OrderCancelled event is processed by Dispatch","then":"The driver is unassigned and notified that the pickup is cancelled"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Race condition: driver picks up package at the exact moment user cancels","expected_behavior":"The system must use optimistic locking or a compare-and-swap on order status. If the status changed between the user loading the cancel screen and submitting, the cancellation is rejected with 'Order status has changed. Please refresh and try again.' No partial state allowed."},{"scenario":"Cancellation during payment processing (refund fails)","expected_behavior":"The order transitions to 'cancelled' regardless of refund outcome. Refund failure is handled asynchronously — the system retries refund and flags for manual review if retries exhaust. The user sees 'Order cancelled. Refund is being processed.' Never block cancellation on refund success."},{"scenario":"Multiple rapid cancellation requests (double-click, network retry)","expected_behavior":"Idempotent handling per BR-8. First request processes; subsequent identical requests return the same success response. Only one OrderCancelled event is emitted. Only one refund is initiated."},{"scenario":"User cancels, then support re-opens the same order","expected_behavior":"Out of scope for self-service cancellation. If business requires order reinstatement, it is a separate bounded context operation performed by support with audit trail. Cancelled is a terminal state for user-facing operations."},{"scenario":"Partial order cancellation (multi-package order, cancel one item)","expected_behavior":"The PM spec says 'cancel their order' — singular, whole order. Partial cancellation is a separate feature. If attempted, the system should not support it in this iteration. Escalated as ambiguity below."},{"scenario":"Driver has been assigned but is unreachable (phone off, app offline)","expected_behavior":"Cancellation proceeds for the order. Dispatch handles driver notification with retries and escalation to dispatch ops. The user's cancellation is not blocked by driver reachability — that is Dispatch's problem, not Order Management's."},{"scenario":"Order placed with a promotional discount or voucher","expected_behavior":"Refund should restore the original payment amount, and the voucher/promo policy determines whether the voucher is re-credited. This is Billing context's responsibility, triggered by the OrderCancelled event. Order Management does not decide voucher policy."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested just before the cancellation window closes (e.g., driver is about to be assigned)","expected_behavior":"The cancellation window is determined by current order status at the moment of processing, not at the moment of request. If status transitions between request and processing, the new status governs applicable rules (fees, eligibility)."},{"scenario":"System outage during cancellation (request accepted but event not published)","expected_behavior":"Use transactional outbox pattern or equivalent. Order status change and event emission must be atomic. If the event cannot be guaranteed, the status change must not commit. The system must never be in a state where the order is cancelled but downstream systems are unaware."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation fees recoup partial operational costs for in-flight orders. Overly restrictive cancellation erodes customer trust and increases chargeback risk. Overly permissive cancellation without fees creates an exploit vector (free same-day courier attempts). Refund timing affects cash flow.","operational":"Each cancellation of an in-flight order wastes driver time and fuel. Dispatch must be notified in real-time to minimize wasted miles. High cancellation rates signal pricing, UX, or delivery-time issues that need root-cause analysis. Support volume will increase if self-service cancellation is too restrictive.","user":"Cancellation is a trust signal — users who can easily cancel are more likely to place orders. A two-step confirmation for fee-bearing cancellations prevents accidental cancellations. Clear communication of fees and refund timelines reduces support contacts and negative reviews.","risk":"Race conditions between cancellation and fulfillment state changes are the primary technical risk. Financial risk from double-refunds if idempotency is not enforced. Regulatory risk in some jurisdictions where cancellation rights are legally mandated (e.g., EU consumer protection — 14-day cooling-off may apply to certain order types).","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (order owners)","impact":"Gain self-service cancellation capability, reducing reliance on support. May incur fees for late cancellations. Need clear communication of cancellation terms at order placement."},{"group":"Drivers / Couriers","impact":"Must be notified promptly when assigned orders are cancelled to avoid wasted trips. Compensation policy for cancelled-after-assignment orders must be defined (not in scope here but a dependency)."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Reduced ticket volume for simple cancellations. Increased responsibility for in-transit cancellation requests that cannot be self-served."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Must handle refund processing, cancellation fee reconciliation, and voucher reinstatement. Needs OrderCancelled events to trigger correct financial workflows."},{"group":"Product / Analytics","impact":"Cancellation reasons feed into product improvement. Cancellation rate becomes a key metric to monitor."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must react to OrderCancelled events to unassign drivers and optimize remaining routes."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The PM spec is a single sentence with no definition of order lifecycle states, cancellation policies, fee structures, user roles, or edge case handling. Nearly every business rule above is an assumption based on logistics domain expertise, not explicit requirements. The spec does not clarify: which order states exist, whether partial cancellation is needed, what fees apply, whether receivers can cancel, or what 'order' even means in this system's ubiquitous language. Confidence is low because the spec quality is extremely poor.","high_confidence_areas":["Terminal states (delivered/cancelled) cannot be cancelled — this is a universal domain truth","Only the order owner should be able to cancel — basic access control","Cancellation must notify downstream systems — fundamental to distributed consistency","Idempotency requirement — standard for any state-changing operation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact order lifecycle states — assumed standard courier flow, may differ in this system","Fee structure and cancellation window boundaries — pure policy, not specified","Whether 'user' means sender only, or also receiver","Whether in-transit cancellation should be blocked or handled differently","Multi-package / partial cancellation support","Voucher/promo reinstatement policy"],"assumptions":["Order lifecycle follows: placed → confirmed → assigned → picked_up → in_transit → delivered","Only the sender (order placer) can cancel, not the receiver","Cancellation fees exist for in-flight orders as an operational cost recovery mechanism","Self-service cancellation is blocked for in_transit status","A cancellation reason is required","The system uses event-driven architecture for cross-context communication"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define which order statuses exist or at which points cancellation is permitted. The entire cancellation policy depends on the order lifecycle, which is undefined.","affected_stakeholders":["Product","Engineering","Operations"],"options":["A) Assume standard courier lifecycle (placed → confirmed → assigned → picked_up → in_transit → delivered)","B) Request PM to explicitly define the order state machine and cancellation eligibility per state"],"recommendation":"B — Request the PM to define the order lifecycle explicitly. Incorrect assumptions here cascade into wrong fee logic, wrong UX flows, and wrong dispatch behavior."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Users' is undefined. Does this mean only the sender (order placer), or can the receiver also cancel? In some logistics systems, receivers can refuse/cancel delivery.","affected_stakeholders":["Product","Customers","Drivers"],"options":["A) Only order placer can cancel","B) Both sender and receiver can cancel with different rules"],"recommendation":"A for initial scope, but PM must confirm. Receiver cancellation has fundamentally different business rules and should be a separate feature if needed."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No cancellation fee policy is defined. Should there be fees? At what thresholds? What amounts?","affected_stakeholders":["Finance","Customers","Product"],"options":["A) No fees ever — full refund always","B) Fees after driver assignment","C) Fees after package pickup","D) Configurable fee tiers per status"],"recommendation":"D — Make fee structure configurable per order status. But PM and Finance must define the initial values."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No mention of partial cancellation for multi-item or multi-package orders. Is an 'order' always a single delivery, or can it contain multiple packages/stops?","affected_stakeholders":["Product","Engineering","Operations"],"options":["A) Order is always atomic — cancel all or nothing","B) Support partial cancellation of individual items/packages"],"recommendation":"A for v1, but need PM confirmation. Partial cancellation is significantly more complex."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No regulatory context provided. In EU and other jurisdictions, consumers may have legally mandated cancellation rights (e.g., 14-day cooling-off period) that override business policy.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal","Compliance","Product"],"options":["A) Implement only business policy, handle legal requirements later","B) Consult legal before defining cancellation terms"],"recommendation":"B — Legal review before launch if operating in regulated markets. Non-compliance risk is high."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier context. A user (sender or potentially receiver) requests to cancel a previously placed delivery order. Cancellation has cascading effects on dispatch, driver assignment, package handling, billing, and refunds. The PM spec is extremely vague — it does not define WHO can cancel, WHEN cancellation is allowed, what constitutes an 'order,' or what happens to in-flight physical processes.","bounded_context":"Order Manag
